cases.description: Basic code point beyond the 7-bit ASCII/basic-Latin range (U+00E9 'e with acute')
Round trip: UNICODE() extracts the code point of an accented character, UNICHAR() converts it straight back
cases.expected_note: UNICODE(char) then UNICHAR(that number) must reproduce the original character exactly

cases.description: Code point beyond the Basic Multilingual Plane (U+1F600 GRINNING FACE emoji), requiring a UTF-16 surrogate pair internally
cases.expected_note: 128512 decimal = U+1F600, the grinning-face emoji; modern Excel supports supplementary-plane code points via surrogate pairs

cases.description: UNICHAR(0) is documented to raise #VALUE! (0 is not a valid character code point). Verified via https://support.microsoft.com/en-us/office/unichar-function-ffeb64f5-f131-44c6-b332-5cd72f0659b8 ("Number: ... Numbers <=0 ... UNICHAR returns the #VALUE! error value")
Negative code point is out of range -> #VALUE! per the same UNICHAR documentation as UNICHAR_zero_error
